---
title: "Lab 01 - Finding and Evaluating an Open-Source Dataset - INSTRUCTIONS"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

# Overview 

In this lab, you will identify, download, and evaluate an open-source dataset that will be used throughout future assignments in the course. 
The purpose of this lab is to familiarize you with publicly available data sources, understand the structure and quality of real-world datasets, and begin thinking critically about how data can be used for analysis and visualization.

You are encouraged to select a dataset related to crime, public safety, transportation, demographics, public health, or another social science topic of interest. 
The dataset selected in this lab will be used in subsequent labs involving data wrangling, visualization, and dashboard development.
Keep this in mind as you search because you will need a dataset that is rich enough to support continued analysis throughout the course.

## Examples

You may locate data from any reputable public source. 
Some examples include:  

  - Data.gov
  - City or county open data portals
  - FBI Crime Data Explorer
  - CDC Wonder
  - Census Bureau data
  - Kaggle (public/open datasets only)
  - GitHub repositories
  - OpenStreetMap or transit datasets
  - City open data portals

## Lab Tasks

### Part 1: Locate a Dataset

Find an open-source dataset that interests you and is appropriate for analysis in this course.

Your dataset should:  

  - Contain at least 100 observations (rows)
  - Include multiple variables (columns)
  - Be downloadable in a usable format (CSV preferred)
  - Have enough complexity to support future cleaning and visualization exercises

### Part 2: Describe the Dataset

Prepare a short written summary (approximately 1–2 pages) addressing the following: 

  - What is the name of the dataset?
  - Where did the data come from? (Include a direct URL to the dataset or repository)
  - Why was the dataset collected?
  - What organization maintains it?
  - What does each row represent?
  - Identify at least three important variables in the dataset and briefly describe what each variable measures.
  - What format is the data stored in?
  - List two or three questions that could be explored using the dataset.
  - Are there missing values, inconsistent formatting, or other issues?
  - What challenges might arise when cleaning or analyzing the data?

# How to Submit

Upload a `.pdf` or `.docx` file to the appropriate link for this assignment on the Canvas page for this course.
